notes: UPlay and game were installed through Steam with no issues. Performance is pretty good. The only real issue is the game will periodically crash after some time (30 minutes or so) The crashing has only occurred during cinematics.  Disabled ESYNC and the Uplay overlay for the best performance.

title: Tom Clancy’s Splinter Cell Blacklist

notes: The game runs fine after disabling uplay overlay, disabling Esync and switching Uplay to offline mode. The issue of the game crashing every 30 minutes or so is because Ubisoft doesn't give you a valid game key by default (which is why DRM triggers after 30 minutes) and you have to get in contact with Ubisoft's tech support and request a valid activation key to be linked to your account. This isn't linux fault because I've seen a lot of Windows users suffering this, so it's working just as natively. The easyest solution is to run Uplay in offline mode so it doesn't validate for a valid key on the servers (multiplayer is dead anyway). Another thing I noticed is that having Uplay in online mode takes a lot of resources from the system so much it makes the game run very slow and stuttery. Is Ubisoft mining cryptocurencies or what?
title: Tom Clancy’s Splinter Cell Blacklist

notes: It works fine, with some weird hitches when loading a level, but anywhere else, it worked rather flawlessly. I couldn't verify the multiplayer at the time, but it didn't connect to other players on Windows anyway. The Uplay overlay is downright broken, refusing to appear after pressing the button combination. DirectX 9 won't work due to Uplay not starting when using either DXVK or Wine's OpenGL implementation of DirectX 9. While Uplay will work on both implementations of DirectX 11, the game refuses to load on DXVK, but runs fine using OpenGL. MSAA cannot be used, it introduces graphical glitches on objects that are further away from the camera. It's recommended to use V-Sync if performance gets too slow. FXAA can be considered, but isn't necessary.
title: Tom Clancy’s Splinter Cell Blacklist

notes: my xbox one controller is not working, other than that, graphics are good and overall game play is good
title: Tom Clancy’s Splinter Cell Blacklist

notes: Works great for about 10-15min, then crashes. Do not select Ultra quality, crash on startup. Neither Large Address Aware nor Very High Priority (increase RAM allocation by only 100MB) help.
title: Tom Clancy’s Splinter Cell Blacklist

notes: Do not check "Remember Me" on Uplay. On second launch, select "Always Start Offline". No more 15/30 minute crashes.
title: Tom Clancy’s Splinter Cell Blacklist
